title: CI: systest: safer random_rfc1918_subnet
body:
Our aarch64 CI system uses 172.31.0.0/20. Because I was (and am)
lazy, my random_rfc1918_subnet() helper was only checking /24.
This causes flakes.

Solution is to actually do it right: binary arithmetic, prefix
matching. This is effectively impossible in bash, so, use a
hairy perl oneliner and add copious tests.

Note: I considered `ipcalc`, `ipcalc-ng`, `sipcalc`, various python packages. Given our system-test constraints where we need to run on fedora, debian, and RHEL, I believe this is the safest approach in terms of portability. There may be cleaner options I did not consider, so please speak up if you have better suggestions.

Fixes: #18693

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Forgot to mention: I've tested 6 CI runs last night and this morning using #17831 and #20161
